Name: Weedle
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Often found in forests and grasslands. It has a sharp, toxic barb of around two inches on top of its head.

Name: Kakuna
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Kakuna and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Almost incapable of moving, this Pokémon can only harden its shell to protect itself when it is in danger.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Kakuna during this turn, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

Name: Exeggcute
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Its six eggs use telepathy to communicate among themselves. It is believed to carry plant genes and the genes of other species.

Name: Cacnea
Effects: If this Pokémon has any [D] Energy attached to it, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It lives in arid locations. Its yellow flowers bloom once a year.

Name: Cacturne
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 60 more damage.
Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It becomes active at night, seeking prey that is exhausted from the day’s desert heat.

Name: Karrablast
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may discard a Shelmet from your hand. If you do, search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: These mysterious Pokémon evolve when they receive electrical stimulation while they are in the same place as Shelmet.

Name: Shelmet
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it and Karrablast are together, and both receive electrical stimulation, they both evolve.

Name: Accelgor
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon and heal all damage from it.
Flavor-Text: Having removed its heavy shell, it becomes very light and can fight with ninja-like movements.

Name: Skiddo
Flavor-Text: Thought to be one of the first Pokémon to live in harmony with humans, it has a placid disposition.

Name: Gogoat
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks do 80 more damage to your opponent’s [G] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It can tell how its trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in the grip on its horns. This empathetic sense lets them run as if one being.

Name: Alolan Marowak
Effects: For each Energy attached to your opponent’s Pokémon, attach a [R] Energy card from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 70 damage for each heads. If either of them is heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The bones it possesses were once its mother’s. Its mother’s regrets have become like a vengeful spirit protecting this Pokémon.

Name: Numel
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its humped back stores intensely hot magma. In rain, the magma cools, slowing its movement.

Name: Camerupt
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: The volcanoes on its back have a major eruption every 10 years—or whenever it becomes really angry.

Name: Staryu
Flavor-Text: Large numbers of these Pokémon make their home at the seaside. At night, a strange red glow radiates from the center of their bodies.

Name: Starmie
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Flavor-Text: Its shining core is thought to receive and transmit enigmatic signals. It has been known to cause headaches in those who approach it.

Name: Magikarp
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is on your Bench, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks (both yours and your opponent’s).
Effects: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Although weak and helpless, this Pokémon is incredibly fertile. They exist in such multitudes, you’ll soon grow tired of seeing them.

Name: Gyarados-GX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.
Discard an Energy from each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Swinub
Flavor-Text: It rubs its snout on the ground to find and dig up food. It sometimes discovers hot springs.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: This Pokémon is now Asleep. Heal 90 damage from it.
Flavor-Text: Although its legs are short, its rugged hooves prevent it from slipping, even on icy ground.

Name: Mamoswine
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 more damage for each heads.
You may put up to 9 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.
Flavor-Text: A frozen Mamoswine was dug from ice dating back 10,000 years. This Pokémon has been around a long, long, long time.

Name: Remoraid
Flavor-Text: It has superb accuracy. The water it shoots out can strike even moving prey from more than 300 feet.

Name: Octillery
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent’s next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
You may discard a Special Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It has a tendency to want to be in holes. It prefers rock crags or pots and sprays ink from them before attacking.

Name: Corphish
Flavor-Text: No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

Name: Crawdaunt
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is a ruffian that uses its pincers to pick up and toss out other Pokémon from its pond.

Name: Feebas
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: A tough Pokémon that is perfectly fine even in dirty water. However, due to its ragged, shabby appearance, it isn’t popular.

Name: Milotic
Effects: Shuffle 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all cards attached to it into their deck.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Milotic has provided inspiration to many artists. It has even been referred to as the most beautiful Pokémon of all.

Name: Regice
Ability-Effect: If you have Regirock in play, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon by your opponent’s Stage 2 Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have slept in a glacier for thousands of years. Its body can’t be melted, even by magma.

Name: Shellos
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: For the most part, it makes its home along the seashore. Its color and form differ according to its habitat and the quality of its food.

Name: Pikachu
Effects: Draw a card.
Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.
Flavor-Text: A plan was recently announced to gather many Pikachu and make an electric power plant.

Name: Alolan Raichu
Ability-Effect: If there is any Stadium card in play, this Pokémon has no Retreat Cost.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It only evolves to this form in the Alola region. According to researchers, its diet is one of the causes of this change.

Name: Alolan Geodude
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks during your opponent’s next turn.
Flavor-Text: Its body is a magnetic stone. Iron sand attaches firmly to the portions of its body that are particularly magnetic.

Name: Alolan Graveler
Effects: This Pokémon does 100 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its preferred food is dravite. After it has eaten this mineral, crystals form inside the Pokémon, rising to the surface of part of its body.

Name: Alolan Golem-GX
Effects: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Your opponent can’t play any cards from their hand during their next turn. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Emolga
Effects: Put 3 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched [L] Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It glides using its cape-like membrane. Electrical energy scatters from it, shocking its friends and foes alike.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Should a strange light be seen flickering in an abandoned building, Gastly is lurking there.

Name: Haunter
Effects: Put 2 damage counters on each of your opponent’s Pokémon that has any damage counters on it.
Flavor-Text: It strikes at humans from total darkness. Those licked by its cold tongue grow weaker with each passing day until they die.

Name: Gengar
Ability-Effect: Whenever your opponent attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Should you feel yourself attacked by a sudden chill, it is evidence of an approaching Gengar. There is no escaping it. Give up.

Name: Misdreavus
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: It will use any means necessary to frighten people and absorb their life energy. It practices constantly to hone its skill in causing fear.

Name: Mismagius
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Pokémon Tool, Special Energy, or Stadium cards from their hand during their next turn.
This attack does 20 damage for each card in your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: It appears as if from nowhere—muttering incantations, placing curses, and giving people terrifying visions.

Name: Spoink
Flavor-Text: It bounces constantly, using its tail like a spring. The shock of bouncing keeps its heart beating.

Name: Grumpig
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t be Confused.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Psych Up attack does 60 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It uses black pearls to amplify its psychic power. It does a strange dance to control foes’ minds.

Name: Chimecho
Effects: Your opponent can’t play any Pokémon that has an Ability from their hand during their next turn.
Flavor-Text: It uses the sucker on its head to hang from a tree or from eaves. It can produce seven different tones.

Name: Pumpkaboo
Effects: Choose a random card from your opponent’s hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.
Flavor-Text: The pumpkin body is inhabited by a spirit trapped in this world. As the sun sets, it becomes restless and active.

Name: Gourgeist
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Before doing damage, you may discard any number of Pokémon Tool cards from your Pokémon. This attack does 40 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

Name: Salandit
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
Flavor-Text: It burns its bodily fluids to create a poisonous gas. When its enemies become disoriented from inhaling the gas, it attacks them.

Name: Salazzle
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. Put 4 damage counters instead of 1 on that Pokémon between turns.
Flavor-Text: For some reason, only females have been found. It creates a reverse harem of male Salandit that it lives with.

Name: Oranguru
Effects: Put 3 Pokémon Tool cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Known for its extreme intelligence, this Pokémon will look down on inexperienced Trainers, so it’s best suited to veteran Trainers.

Name: Nihilego-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may leave both Active Pokémon Confused and Poisoned.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Add the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck to their Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mankey
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It can spontaneously become enraged. Everyone near it clears out as it rampages and the resulting loneliness makes it angrier still.

Name: Primeape
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It has been known to become so angry that it dies as a result. Its face looks peaceful in death, however.

Name: Cubone
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When it thinks of its deceased mother, it weeps loudly. Mandibuzz that hear its cries will attack it from the air.

Name: Regirock
Ability-Effect: Your Registeel’s attacks do 10 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Its entire body is made of rock. If any part chips off in battle, it attaches rocks to repair itself.

Name: Gastrodon
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
This attack does 10 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: It has strong regenerative capabilities. Even if parts of it are bitten off by fish Pokémon, it will return to normal within a few hours.

Name: Stufful
Flavor-Text: Despite its adorable appearance, when it gets angry and flails about, its arms and legs could knock a pro wrestler sprawling.

Name: Bewear
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from the attacks of your opponent’s non-[R] Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This immensely dangerous Pokémon possesses overwhelming physical strength. Its habitat is generally off-limits.

Name: Buzzwole-GX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
This attack does 40 damage for each of your remaining Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Houndour
Flavor-Text: Around dawn, its ominous howl echoes through the area to announce that this is its territory.

Name: Houndoom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Burned.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: Long ago, people imagined its eerie howls to be the call of the grim reaper.

Name: Deino
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They cannot see, so they tackle and bite to learn about their surroundings. Their bodies are covered in wounds.

Name: Zweilous
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: The two heads do not get along. Whichever head eats more than the other gets to be the leader.

Name: Hydreigon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may choose 3 of your Benched Pokémon. Then, discard your other Benched Pokémon.
Effects: You may discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The heads on their arms do not have brains. They use all three heads to consume and destroy everything.

Name: Guzzlord-GX
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mawile
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses its docile-looking face to lull foes into complacency, then bites with its huge, relentless jaws.

Name: Aron
Flavor-Text: It usually lives deep in mountains. However, hunger may drive it to eat railroad tracks and cars.

Name: Lairon
Flavor-Text: It loves iron ore. Groups of them fight for territory by bashing one another with their steel bodies.

Name: Aggron
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on all of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: It claims an entire mountain as its own. The more wounds it has, the more it has battled, so don’t take it lightly.

Name: Registeel
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If Regice is on your Bench, heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Tempered by pressure underground over tens of thousands of years, its body cannot be scratched.

Name: Escavalier
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Wearing the shell covering they stole from Shelmet, they defend themselves and attack with two lances.

Name: Kartana-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Take a Prize card. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jigglypuff
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It hugely inflates its stomach and sings a mysterious melody. If you hear this melody, you’ll become sleepy right away.

Name: Wigglytuff
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
If any of your opponent’s Pokémon have any [D] Energy attached to them, this attack does 60 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It sheds its fine fur when the seasons change. The fur is gathered and spun into a luxurious yarn.

Name: Xerneas
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon can’t use Bright Horns during your next turn.
Flavor-Text: Legends say it can share eternal life. It slept for a thousand years in the form of a tree before its revival.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor-GX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Move any number of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Jangmo-o
Flavor-Text: It expresses its feelings by smacking its scales. Metallic sounds echo through the tall mountains where Jangmo-o lives.

Name: Hakamo-o
Effects: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It leaps at its prey with a courageous shout. Its scaly punches tear its opponents to shreds.

Name: Kommo-o
Effects: If you have fewer Pokémon in play than your opponent, this attack does 90 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When it spots enemies, it threatens them by jingling the scales on its tail. Weak opponents will crack and flee in panic.

Name: Miltank
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is your Active Pokémon, whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Pokémon, heal 90 damage from that Pokémon.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its milk is high in calories and packed with nutrients. Therefore, if you drink too much of it, you may wind up with a body like Miltank’s.

Name: Swablu
Flavor-Text: For some reason, it likes to land on people’s heads softly and act like it’s a hat.

Name: Altaria
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a [N] Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It flies gracefully through the sky. Its melodic humming makes you feel like you’re in a dream.

Name: Starly
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
Flavor-Text: They flock in great numbers. Though small, they flap their wings with great power.

Name: Staravia
Flavor-Text: They maintain huge flocks, although fierce scuffles break out between various flocks.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by the damage from this attack, switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The muscles in its wings and legs are strong. It can easily fly while gripping a small Pokémon.

Name: Regigigas
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t attack unless Regirock, Regice, and Registeel are on your Bench.
Effects: Discard any Stadium card in play.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Minccino greet each other by grooming one another thoroughly with their tails.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: Choose 2 cards from your discard pile. Then, ask your opponent if you may put them into your hand. If yes, put those cards into your hand. If no, this attack does 80 damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Their white fur feels amazing to touch. Their fur repels dust and prevents static electricity from building up.

Name: Bunnelby
Flavor-Text: They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through.

Name: Diggersby
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent’s next turn.
Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
Flavor-Text: With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites.

Name: Type: Null
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The heavy control mask it wears suppresses its intrinsic capabilities. This Pokémon has some hidden special power.

Name: Silvally-GX
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Counter Catcher
Description: You can play this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Dashing Pouch
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to discards Energy for its Retreat Cost, put that Energy into your hand instead of the discard pile.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Devoured Field
Description: The attacks of [D] Pokémon and [N] Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) do 10 more damage to the opponent’s Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Fighting Memory
Description: The Silvally-GX this card is attached to is a [F] Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Gladion
Description: Look at your face-down Prize cards and put 1 of them into your hand. Then, shuffle this Gladion into your remaining Prize cards and put them back face down. If you didn’t play this Gladion from your hand, it does nothing.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lusamine
Description: Put 2 in any combination of Supporter and Stadium cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Peeking Red Card
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand. You may have your opponent count the cards in their hand, shuffle those cards into their deck, then draw that many cards.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Psychic Memory
Description: The Silvally-GX this card is attached to is a [P] Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Sea of Nothingness
Description: Special Conditions are not removed when Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) evolve or devolve.
Stadium-Rule: This card stays in play when you play it. Discard this card if another Stadium card comes into play. If another card with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Counter Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy.If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, and if this card is attached to a Pokémon that isn’t a Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 2 Energy at a time.

Name: Gyarados-GX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.
Discard an Energy from each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alolan Golem-GX
Effects: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Your opponent can’t play any cards from their hand during their next turn. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may leave both Active Pokémon Confused and Poisoned.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Add the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck to their Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Buzzwole-GX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
This attack does 40 damage for each of your remaining Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Guzzlord-GX
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kartana-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Take a Prize card. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor-GX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Move any number of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Silvally-GX
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gladion
Description: Look at your face-down Prize cards and put 1 of them into your hand. Then, shuffle this Gladion into your remaining Prize cards and put them back face down. If you didn’t play this Gladion from your hand, it does nothing.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Lusamine
Description: Put 2 in any combination of Supporter and Stadium cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Olivia
Description: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon-GX, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Gyarados-GX
Effects: If there is any Stadium card in play, this attack does 100 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium card.
Discard an Energy from each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alolan Golem-GX
Effects: This Pokémon does 50 damage to itself.
Your opponent can’t play any cards from their hand during their next turn. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Nihilego-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may leave both Active Pokémon Confused and Poisoned.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can’t retreat during your opponent’s next turn.
Add the top 2 cards of your opponent’s deck to their Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Buzzwole-GX
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
This Pokémon can’t attack during your next turn.
This attack does 40 damage for each of your remaining Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Guzzlord-GX
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kartana-GX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Effects: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.
Take a Prize card. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Alolan Exeggutor-GX
Effects: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to this Pokémon to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Move any number of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Silvally-GX
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon in play have no Retreat Cost.
Effects: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Counter Catcher
Description: You can play this card only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent.Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

Name: Wishful Baton
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is your Active Pokémon and is Knocked Out by damage from an opponent’s attack, move up to 3 basic Energy cards from that Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached to it.

Name: Counter Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy.If you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent, and if this card is attached to a Pokémon that isn’t a Pokémon-GX or Pokémon-EX, this card provides every type of Energy but provides only 2 Energy at a time.

Name: Warp Energy
Description: This card provides [C] Energy.When you attach this card from your hand to your Active Pokémon, switch that Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Name: Guzzlord-GX
Effects: Discard the top 5 cards of your deck. If any of those cards are Energy cards, attach them to this Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from this attack, take 2 more Prize cards. (You can’t use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)
GX-Rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Regigigas
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon can’t attack unless Regirock, Regice, and Registeel are on your Bench.
Effects: Discard any Stadium card in play.
Flavor-Text: It is said to have made Pokémon that look like itself from a special ice mountain, rocks, and magma.

Name: Type: Null
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The heavy control mask it wears suppresses its intrinsic capabilities. This Pokémon has some hidden special power.
